required_sections.detail: All required sections present and non-trivial. Section 3 (Learning Objectives): 6 objectives, ~120 words — passes ≥50w. Section 4 (Introduction): ~200 words — passes ≥50w. Section 5 (Core Concepts): 5 subsections with ~700 words total — passes ≥100w. Section 10 (Key Takeaways): 6 bullets, ~155 words — passes ≥50w.

citation_density.sections_5_6_7_density: Estimated >60% of standalone factual assertions in sections 5, 6, 7 carry a [N] citation marker. Primary resource [1] cited ≥1 time. No fabricated citations detected.
citation_density.detail: Strict mode (web_search source) satisfied. All primary resources cited at least once.

sequence_drift.prior_concepts_introduced: All prior topics (1.1–12.14) have empty concepts_introduced arrays in sequence.index.json. Cross-refs Python and Anthropic API established via prerequisites 12.10 and 12.11.

sequence_drift.detail: All deferred concepts appear as bare names in ≤1 sentence with a 'covered later' pointer. No forward concept is defined or taught in this corpus.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~2,320 words against standard profile minimum of 3,000. Shortfall is ~22.7% below minimum, which is within the 30% buffer zone (hard-fail threshold = 2,100 words). Does not hard-fail. Soft finding: corpus is lighter than standard depth warrants — builder should consider expanding Core Concepts or Real-World Patterns to reach ~3,000 words before artifact generation.

topic_scope.detail: Headline scope: system prompt vs user prompt (context-setting vs live request). All content in Core Concepts, Implementation, and Real-World Patterns stays within this boundary. Pattern 3 (enforcing output format via system prompt) is in-scope as it demonstrates a direct application of the system prompt's role. 'Prompt injection' mentioned once with explicit future-topic deferral. 'Role assignment' named-and-deferred in intro. No concept is taught beyond the headline boundary.

findings.detail: GDPR appears unexplained in one example user-prompt quote in section 5.4: 'What are the reporting requirements for small businesses under GDPR?' It is inside a quoted string (illustrative example), not instructional prose. This is the only unexplained acronym — below the ≥2 soft-fail threshold.

beginner_readability.detail: LLM spelled out on first use in section 3 ('LLM (Large Language Model)'). API is treated as prior knowledge via prerequisites 12.10/12.11. All new terms (system prompt, user prompt, authority, precedence, static context, guardrails) are defined with a bold term + plain-language meaning on first use. No paragraph introduces 3+ undefined terms simultaneously. Sentences are short and active. No hedging phrases detected. One soft finding (GDPR in an example string) — does not escalate to hard fail (<3 distinct readability findings).

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. One soft finding: word count (~2,320 words) is below the standard profile minimum of 3,000 words but above the 30%-outside hard-fail threshold of 2,100 words; builder should expand before artifact generation. One minor soft readability finding: GDPR unexplained in an illustrative example string (section 5.4). No forward references, no scope creep, citation density satisfactory, required sections complete.
